{"key_event":"Catholic Association and O’Connell’s Clare victory","reasoning":"Civil society strengthened noticeably as industrial urban organization, cheap print, trade-union legalization in limited form, dissenting inclusion, and especially Irish Catholic mass mobilization showed capacity to force issues onto Westminster despite repression. State power rose only slightly: Peterloo, the Six Acts, surveillance, prosecutions, and Peel-era administrative reform showed strong coercive and bureaucratic capacity, but the unreformed settlement was increasingly constrained by organized popular politics.","society_change":0.6,"society_power":7.0,"state_change":0.1,"state_power":7.6}
